description_long: This is a bronze-colored Corinthian-style helmet with a pronounced nose guard and cheek plates, reminiscent of ancient Greek armor. The helmet features a smooth, rounded dome with a central ridge running from front to back, and a pointed crest at the top. The eye openings are narrow and angular, providing both protection and visibility. Decorative rivets and a thin, raised border accentuate the edges, while a red cloth and several coins are visible beneath the helmet, suggesting it is displayed on a stand. The surface has a slightly weathered, metallic patina, giving it an authentic, aged appearance. No modern branding or graphic designs are present, emphasizing its historical aesthetic.
description: A bronze, ancient Greek-style helmet with a pronounced nose guard, cheek plates, and a weathered metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular eye openings, central nose guard, and symmetrical cheek plates.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the rounded dome, side rivets, and the helmet's pronounced rear flare.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, rounded back and central ridge running from top to bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with visible cheek plate and rear flare, plus the red cloth beneath.
